codeReview.summary: Current repo state still fails this feature review: exchange breadth/ranking diffs are classified as expected, but the same feature also reclassified /global and /exchange_rates breadth findings, which exceeds the exchange-only scope and improves the gate through unrelated divergence-registry drift.

issues.description: The exchange-breadth-targeted-fix feature was scoped to exchange registry/detail/ticker breadth and ranking gaps, but the current registry entries added here also include expected-exchange-rates-envelope-breadth and expected-global-breadth-gap. Reclassifying non-exchange findings in the same change set violates the assigned scope and spills divergence handling into global and exchange-rates surfaces.

The regenerated diff report now marks exchanges-canonical, exchange-detail-binance, and exchange-tickers-binance as expected, but it also marks exchange-rates-canonical and global-canonical as expected under the same feature. That broadens the milestone improvement by unrelated registry drift instead of exchange-only behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The compatibility-hardening-worker procedure does not explicitly require divergence-registry/report updates to stay within the assigned endpoint family for narrowly scoped breadth-fix features. That gap allowed an exchange-only feature to clear unrelated global/exchange-rates findings in the same pass.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission features.json scopes exchange-breadth-targeted-fix to exchange registry/detail/ticker gaps, but commit 227bdae adds expected-exchange-rates-envelope-breadth and expected-global-breadth-gap in data/coingecko-snapshots/divergence-registry.json and the resulting diff report marks exchange-rates-canonical and global-canonical as expected.

summary: Fail: current repository state still handles exchange breadth by expected-divergence classification, but this feature also reclassified unrelated /global and /exchange_rates breadth findings, so it remains out of scope and weakens the improvement-gate evidence.
